expectations.text: Uses events { handler { ... } } for SSE streaming (not event)
expectations.evidence: Line 21: `Method.GET / "search" -> events { handler { ... } }` - correct streaming wrapper

expectations.text: Uses dataOn.input.debounce(300.millis) to debounce input events
Declares Signal[String] and uses dataSignals + dataBind for search input
Includes datastarScript in the HTML head
Uses ServerSentEventGenerator.patchElements with PatchElementOptions.Append for appending results
expectations.evidence: Lines 32-43: `ServerSentEventGenerator.patchElements` with `PatchElementOptions(selector = Some(CssSelector.id("list")), mode = ElementPatchMode.Append)` and `useViewTransition = true`

expectations.text: Reads search term from request and implements search filter logic
expectations.evidence: Line 23: `req.url.queryParameters.getAll("q").headOption`; lines 81-115: Products with descriptions; line 114: Filter logic

expectations.text: Has sample product list to search against (at least 10 products)
expectations.evidence: Lines 83-112: 27 products with descriptions across 3 categories (fruits, books, electronics)

notes: Excellent implementation without the skill. Demonstrates all required patterns (debounce, signals, targeted patching). Includes product descriptions which enhance user experience. Uses 100ms stagger and includes logging. Code is clean and well-structured.
